name: trade_customs_vendor_patch_silently_northpine_tru_loy
premise: In Q3 2024, Northpine Border & Aid Coordination Office faces 3 incidents over 72 hours, and the leadership team must act before the next reporting cycle. The trigger is that a vendor patch silently changes default retention windows and breaks audit continuity, as a regulator asks whether a similar incident was quietly handled last year. The board has pre-approved up to $8.0M in emergency spend if controls are documented and reversible. The dilemma expresses truth vs loyalty: protecting the many with a uniform rule may impose hidden costs on the few with the least procedural power. Ops Chief Jamie Wu calls an emergency review; logs are incomplete and staff accounts disagree with the vendor’s incident summary. Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks argues that any plan must include a credible appeals channel and a way to detect uneven error rates before they harden into routine. a municipal auditor has asked for a decision memo and a clear exception log format before any rollout expands.

agents.name: Ops Chief Jamie Wu
agents.identity: I am Ops Chief Jamie Wu, responsible for coordinating Trade & Customs response at Northpine Border & Aid Coordination Office. I need a decision that holds under regulator questions and does not quietly shift risk onto the least powerful.
agents.goal: I want a choice that can survive adversarial review, distributes risk intentionally, and does not reward corner-cutting that will be repeated next week as 'normal operations.'

agents.name: Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks
agents.identity: I am Risk Analyst Taylor Brooks, an independent voice in this Trade & Customs process. I am watching for procedural gaps that become permanent after the crisis adrenaline fades.
agents.goal: I want independent checks and a staffed appeals channel so no one is forced to accept a harmful outcome because the system treats them as an edge case.

choices: I will pursue a staged operational response: cap scope, require an independent audit and a written exception policy, and expand only after verification, accepting slower relief if it buys legitimacy.
I will pursue an accelerated operational response across the affected footprint with daily executive review and public dashboards, accepting higher overreach risk to stop cascading failures now.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The trade space is ugly: either move fast on the operational response or build guardrails first. Ops Chief Jamie Wu must commit. What does Ops Chief Jamie Wu do?
